title: US Strikes Kharg Island (90% of Iran Oil Exports); Trump Issues Apocalyptic Deadline: 'A Whole Civilization Will Die Tonight'
detail: US forces struck Kharg Island overnight into April 7 — the offshore terminal that handles approximately 90% of Iran's crude oil export capacity — as part of a new escalation phase in Operation Epic Fury (Day 39). Dozens of military targets on the island were struck in coordinated waves. Iran's railway bridge in Kashan was also struck, killing at least 2 people. Iranian Red Crescent reported strikes on a residential area in Tehran. Trump issued a new ultimatum with an 8:00 p.m. ET deadline: Iran must fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz or the United States would 'destroy every bridge and power plant in Iran within four hours.' Trump posted to Truth Social: 'A whole civilization will die tonight, never to be brought back again.' Iran's deputy foreign minister called on Iranian youth to form human chains around power plants and critical infrastructure in a show of defiance. Iran's military dismissed Trump's threats as 'delusional.' Iran responded by announcing its restraint on targeting regional oil infrastructure (Gulf Arab states' facilities) 'would no longer apply,' raising the specter of broader regional energy war. Oil prices surged on the Kharg Island strikes and the threat to regional energy infrastructure. Iran had countered the ceasefire proposal with a 10-point permanent ceasefire framework — rejected by Trump. The situation represented the most acute escalation phase of the Iran war.

media.caption: US strikes Kharg Island as Trump issues apocalyptic 8pm deadline — Iran threatens regional oil infrastructure in response

title: DOGE Causes SSA Server Crash After Deploying Untested Verification Software; Millions of Beneficiaries Affected
detail: Social Security Administration servers crashed on April 7 after DOGE personnel deployed updated identity verification software without load testing, according to reporting by Gizmodo and multiple technology news outlets. The crash compounded ongoing SSA operational failures following the agency's roughly 7,500 workforce reductions under DOGE-directed staffing cuts. The SSA had already been struggling with backlogs and processing delays as a direct result of the staffing reductions. The crash affected claims processing and beneficiary access to online services. The incident drew sharp condemnation from Democratic lawmakers and senior citizens' advocacy groups, who argued DOGE's intervention in Social Security systems was reckless and put vulnerable Americans at risk. The SSA outage added to a growing list of government IT and service failures attributed to DOGE's rapid and poorly tested technology deployments across federal agencies. The administration had claimed DOGE was modernizing federal IT infrastructure, but critics pointed to the SSA crash as evidence of rushed, undercooked implementations.

media.caption: DOGE Causes SSA Server Crash After Deploying Untested Verification Software; Millions of Beneficiaries Affected

title: Trump to Pursue Stability with China's Xi Jinping at May Summit; USTR Greer Confirms Diplomatic Channel Amid Tariff War
detail: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer confirmed on April 7 that President Trump intends to pursue a stability framework with Chinese President Xi Jinping at a planned May summit, even as the US-China tariff war escalates. Greer described the planned meeting as a direct diplomatic channel to manage tensions, with the US side seeking to stabilize economic relations rather than pursue further escalation. The announcement came days after China's 34% retaliatory tariff on all US goods took effect and Trump imposed 145% cumulative tariffs on Chinese imports, triggering fears of a full trade decoupling. The planned Trump-Xi meeting represents the first direct presidential-level engagement between the two leaders since the tariff war escalated sharply in April 2026.

media.caption: USTR Greer confirms Trump-Xi stability meeting planned for May amid tariff war escalation

title: Congressional Democrats Demand Emergency Recess Recall to Vote on Halting Trump's Iran War
detail: Congressional Democrats formally demanded that Republican leaders cut short the congressional recess and reconvene for an emergency session to vote on halting Trump's Iran war. The demand marks a significant legislative challenge to executive war powers, with Democrats arguing Congress must reassert its constitutional authority over the use of force under the War Powers Resolution. The push came as US forces conducted major strikes on Iranian infrastructure — including Kharg Island, which handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports — and Trump issued ultimatums to Iran with an 8pm ET deadline. Democratic leaders argued the war powers challenge must be addressed by the full legislature rather than left to executive discretion during a congressional recess.

title: New 50% Metal Tariff Rules Take Effect: Steel, Aluminum, Copper Derivatives Face Full-Value Duties
detail: Trump's revised metal tariff proclamation from April 2 took full effect on April 6–7: goods made almost entirely of aluminum, steel, or copper now face a 50% tariff on their full value; derivative articles substantially made of these metals face a 25% tariff. The new rules replaced the tiered tariff structure that had been partially invalidated by the Supreme Court's February 2026 IEEPA ruling. By restructuring metal tariffs through Section 232 authority rather than IEEPA, the administration sought to insulate the new duties from judicial challenge. US manufacturers, particularly in the automotive, aerospace, and appliance sectors, warned the 50% raw material tariff would be passed on to consumers and could accelerate production shifts overseas. The metal tariff changes came simultaneously with China's 34% retaliatory tariff taking effect April 10 — raising fears of a full-scale trade decoupling between the world's two largest economies. The Peterson Institute for International Economics estimated the combined tariff burden on US households would rise to approximately $2,000/year by mid-2026.
